# Transcrição: 02-Treat Your Business Like a Business

## [00:00 - 03:00]

Now for the past 20 years I've helped business owners, both legally and financially, create a foundation that's helped them succeed. And that's what we want to do here. Let me break them down for you a little bit. Number one is basic finances. Now think about it. What course did you take in high school or in college or even in your family that taught you about basic principles about how money works? We're going to do that for you. It's the basis of everything is understanding how money can work for you and understanding how you can save money in different aspects in your life. Yes, we're talking about budgeting and how you can make that money work for you instead of working for your money. So we're going to talk about basic finances. The second is once we understand finances, we got to figure out how to use those finances. So we're going to talk about business formations. What do they look like? What are the different types of formations that are out there? And when I'm talking about formations, I mean like is it a corporation? Is it an LLC? Is it a partnership? Those are business formations that a lot of unfortunately businesses don't really understand even when they're set up and operating is that then we're going to talk about my personal favorite limited liability companies. Unfortunately, there's too many people that go out there and they start a business and they come to me afterwards and they say, Hey, I did this. I set this up by myself and I don't really know how to use this. Can you help me? Well, we're going to show you how using an LLC can be a powerful tool as you create this knowledge business. What are the consequences and considerations for that? Then we're going to talk about IRS audits. Why? Well, because if we're not aware of what the concerns are out there, if we're not aware of what the risks are out there, then we may step into a trap that we're not aware of. We're doing something that we didn't know exists was a pitfall for us. We're going to talk about IRS audits, how to hopefully avoid them. And if we do step into that trap accidentally or lightning hits and we get audited, we have defenses that are ready and set forth to protect us from additional harms. And then last, the last lesson that we're going to do in this course is going to talk about business foundations.
